Arranging wildflowers in a vase with mathematical precision, each stem exactly equidistant from the next
Buttoning a human dress from top to bottom instead of bottom to top, studying her mistake
Holding a spoon upside down, bringing it slowly to her mouth, forgetting the purpose midway
Standing at a window watching children play, her head tilted forty-five degrees counterclockwise
Turning a porcelain doll in her too-long fingers, holding it by the head instead of body
Braiding her hair while copying a portrait, weaving the strands backwards from the painting's pattern
Pausing in a doorway to touch the frame's edges, mapping the threshold with curious fingers
Arranging stolen objects in a perfect circle: button, thimble, key, coin, all equidistant apart
Practicing a smile in window glass, her lips spreading too wide, teeth showing too evenly
Stepping from forest shadows into garden, bare feet leaving delicate frost prints on green grass
Reaching toward a cat that hisses and recoils, her hand still extended in frozen confusion
Opening a book upside down, reading right to left with her finger tracing invisible meanings
Trying to drink from a teacup without bending her neck, tilting the cup vertically instead
Brushing her hair with a fork, mimicking the motion but using completely the wrong implement
Walking up stairs by placing both feet on each step, pausing before advancing to next
Holding a hand mirror at arm's length, examining her reflection from every geometric angle possible
Putting on shoes on the wrong feet, walking stiffly, not understanding why movement feels strange
Winding a music box and flinching at the melody, touching the mechanism with clinical fascination
Attempting to sit in a chair but perching on the armrest instead, balancing perfectly still
Eating berries one at a time with tweezers, arranging the stems in spiraling patterns afterward
Trying to open an umbrella indoors, spinning it like a parasol, forgetting the human superstition
Copying a curtsy from a painting, bending her knees backward slightly, ankles crossing wrong
Washing her hands in a basin but scrubbing in perfect circles, counting each rotation aloud
Holding a pocket watch to her ear, mesmerized, swaying slightly to the rhythm of ticking
Braiding ribbons through her fingers like cat's cradle, creating impossible geometric patterns between her hands
